content: Is kernelCI able to run on patchsets before they are applied? Thought I saw that somewhere

You are probably thinking of the kernel test robot. Yeah, they pick patches from the list (and also when there are updates to registered trees) and report if they spot a problem.

I requested about a month ago that they introduce building and running the Rust KUnit tests. They said they will! :)
Right after I asked that, kernelCI replied to the patchset :laughing: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/202407271842.ujYe0mc0-lkp@intel.com/

Right after I asked that, kernelCI replied to the patchset

I don't think that is considered KernelCI (though maybe they send the results to KernelCI), i.e. that is the kernel test robot. You can ask them for branches/lists to be monitored (or ignored).

Are the patches they test handpicked?

I think they intend to test all patches that can be applied cleanly and can enable Rust (back then they told me "Theoretically we should be testing all the patches that Rust can be enabled.")
If kernel test robot picks up everything on the list and replies to it, how would kernelCI be different? I assumed they were parts of the same thing.
7 messages were moved here from #General > Spinlocks with IRQs? by Trevor Gross.
And do any / will any of these services check rustfmt and clippy?

KernelCI is a foundation + several projects (including a database of results and a dashboard), and they watch trees (at least that is what I used it for in the past a bit, but I think it can be triggered in other ways too: https://docs.kernelci.org/architecture/). They are working on a new dashboard nowadays.
I would like to use it more in the future, and we are going to discuss it (and "exotic toolchains" in general :) in LPC this year.

Trevor Gross said:

And do any / will any of these services check rustfmt and clippy?

Yes, I asked them (kernel test robot). They are already running rustfmtcheck, and I think they are running (or they will be) running CLIPPY=1. Also I asked for building the docs.
